components.role: Voltage-controlled resistor — JFET operated in its ohmic/triode region acts as a variable resistor controlled by Vgs
Envelope detector — rectifies and smooths input signal to generate JFET control voltage
Input/series resistor — forms voltage divider with JFET Rds to create variable attenuation
Envelope smoothing — sets attack/release time constant
Attack time resistor — in series with diode for charging Cenv
Release time resistor — discharge path for Cenv
Sustain/sensitivity control — scales the control voltage applied to the JFET gate
Input coupling — blocks DC from JFET drain

dsp_model.description: Envelope-controlled variable attenuator; JFET Rds changes with gate voltage to create compression

parameters.distortion_note: JFET VCR adds second-harmonic distortion proportional to signal amplitude

behavioral_description: A JFET operated in its ohmic region as a voltage-controlled resistor, forming a variable attenuator for compression. The JFET's drain-source resistance (Rds) varies smoothly from a few hundred ohms (gate at 0V) to near-infinite (gate at pinch-off), controlled by the envelope-detected signal at the gate. This creates a natural, organic compression with a very soft knee — the transition from unity gain to compression is gradual and musical. The JFET VCR introduces slight second-harmonic distortion that increases with signal level, which adds warmth and character that many players prefer over cleaner VCA-based compressors. This topology can also accept an external CV input, making it useful as a voltage-controlled attenuator for tremolo, ducking, or sidechain effects. The compression character is similar to optical compressors — smooth and unobtrusive.
